Han Dang, styledYigong, was fromLingzhi[令支],Liaoxi[遼西].A
He was good at shooting while riding a horse, and he had physicalstrength.B
Ling [令] has the fanqie of Lang [郎] and Ding [定]; Zhi [支] has the fanqie of Ju [巨] and Er [兒].
TheBook of Wustates: Through bitter labor,[Han] Dangrendered merits, but because he was a military servant, it was given to the heroic and mighty, and he thus was not given peerage or title. Only during the end of the time of[Sun] Jianwas he madeMajor of a Separate Unit.
He was favored bySun Jian, and he followed in conquests and military campaigns. He found himself in perilous situations several times, charging into the enemies and capturing prisoners. He was madeMajor of a Separate Unit. WhenSun Ceferried across to the east, he followed in the suppression of three commanderies.1He was promoted toColonel who Ascends First, given two thousand troops, and five hundred cavalry. He followed in the conquest ofLiu Xun, the defeat ofHuang Zu, and returned to suppressPoyang[鄱陽], becomingChiefofLe’an[樂安長]. The Shanyue submitted out of awe. Later, he, asHousehold General, resisted and defeatedExcellency Caoalong withZ... (truncated)
